steps.title: Identify the core principle
steps.content: The passage establishes that material in slower-moving dust (SPC, AST) has lower ablation rates than the same material in faster-moving dust (HTC, OCC). The example gives iron from AST at 28% and asks for a completion that illustrates this principle.

steps.title: Apply the principle to the data
steps.content:
Iron from HTC dust is 90%, demonstrating the same element from a faster source with dramatically higher ablation.

The other choices either use a different element, stay within slower-moving sources, or fail to show how dust speed affects ablation.

That matches choice C — C is correct.
